{{nihongo|'''Kazahaya no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|風速国造|''kazahaya no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''kazahaya kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled the central portion of [[Iyo Province]] under the later {{ill|ritsuryō provinces|ja|令制国}}, corresponding to the central part of modern [[Ehime Prefecture]].

According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, during the reign of [[Emperor Ōjin]], Asari, a 4th-generation descendant of Ikashikoo-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Mononobe-no-Muraji, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.

The Kazahaya no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the northern area of the later {{ill|Kazahaya District|ja|風早郡}}—that is, of [[Onsen District]]—and its [[descendants]] prospered in the region and grew into a powerful {{ill|local clan|ja|豪族|lt=gōzoku}}. Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine, located at Hattanji, [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]], Ehime Prefecture, is an {{ill|Engishiki shrine|ja|延喜式内社}} said to have been founded by the first kuni no miyatsuko, Mononobe-no-Asari, and it was venerated as the {{ill|tutelary shrine|ja|氏神|lt=ujigami}} of the Kazahaya and Mononobe clans.

== References ==
* Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Hensan Iinkai (ed.), ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten (Ehime-ken)'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1991, ISBN 4-04-001380-8, pp. 26, 192.

{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かさはやのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:伊予国]]
[[Category:愛媛県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:風速国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
